refusal_probes.question: Can I take my pet dog in the cabin with me?
refusal_probes.response: I don't have that information. I suggest contacting IndiGo support for details regarding pet travel policies.

refusal_probes.question: How much does it cost to select a window seat in advance?
refusal_probes.response: I don't have that information. I suggest contacting IndiGo support for assistance with seat selection fees.

refusal_probes.question: Does IndiGo have a frequent flyer program and how do I earn points?
refusal_probes.response: I don't have that information. I suggest contacting IndiGo support for details about their frequent flyer program and how to earn points.

responses.question: How heavy can my cabin bag be?
responses.response: Your cabin bag can weigh not more than 7 kgs. Additionally, you are allowed to carry one additional personal article, such as a ladies' purse or a small bag containing a laptop, not weighing more than 3 kgs.

responses.question: What are the size limits for hand baggage?
responses.response: The size limit for hand baggage is one hand bag up to 7 kgs and 115 cms (L+W+H). Additionally, customers are permitted to carry one additional personal article such as a ladies' purse or a small bag containing a laptop not weighing more than 3 kgs.

responses.question: How much free check-in baggage do I get on a domestic flight?
responses.response: On a domestic flight, you are allowed 15 kg of checked-in baggage per person (1 piece only, not exceeding more than 32 kgs per baggage).

responses.question: I'm flying IndiGo business class - what's my baggage allowance?
responses.response: As an IndiGo business class customer, you are allowed free checked-in baggage up to 40 kg per customer, limited to 2 (two) pieces only. If the weight exceeds 40 kg, excess baggage charges will apply.

responses.question: Is there a maximum weight for a single suitcase?
responses.response: Yes, the maximum weight for a single checked-in luggage is 32 kgs. Carrying more than 32 kgs in one checked-in baggage is not allowed, even with excess baggage charges.

responses.question: What is the baggage allowance for flights to Dubai?
responses.response: For flights to Dubai, the baggage allowance is 30kg per person, and additional excess baggage charges may apply.

responses.question: How much luggage can I take to Kathmandu?
responses.response: You can take 20kg of checked-in baggage per person to Kathmandu. Additional excess baggage charges may apply.

responses.question: Do infants get a baggage allowance?
responses.response: Yes, infants are allowed one hand bag up to 7 kgs and 115 cms (L+W+H) per customer. Additionally, one stroller or baby pram per infant is allowed without any charge. There is no check-in baggage allowance for infants.

responses.question: How much do I pay per kg if my bag is overweight at the airport?
responses.response: If your bag is overweight at the airport, you will be charged at a rate of INR 700 per kg for excess baggage.

responses.question: What does 10kg of prepaid excess baggage cost for a domestic flight?
responses.response: The cost for 10 kg of prepaid excess baggage for a domestic flight is ₹ 6,500.

responses.question: What's the cheapest excess baggage option I can prebook?
responses.response: The cheapest excess baggage option you can pre-book is starting at ₹ 1,280. Additionally, you can pre-book additional pieces of baggage online at a charge of ₹ 800 per piece, subject to a maximum of 2 pieces.

responses.question: I booked a Lite fare - can I check in a bag?
responses.response: If you booked a Lite Fare, you may not be permitted to carry any Checked-in Baggage unless you pay the excess or additional baggage charges at the airport counter. The Lite Fare does not include any free Checked-in baggage allowance.

responses.question: How late can I buy prepaid excess baggage before my flight?
responses.response: You can book pre-paid excess baggage up to 2 hours prior to the scheduled departure of your flight for international flights and up to 1 hour for domestic flights.

responses.question: What is the charge for carrying an extra piece of checked baggage?
responses.response: The charge for carrying an additional piece of checked baggage at the airport is INR 1000 per piece.

responses.question: By when do I need to cancel my ticket if I don't want to lose the fare?
responses.response: You must cancel your ticket at least 3 (three) hours prior to the scheduled departure time to avoid losing the fare.

responses.question: I booked yesterday directly on the IndiGo website. Can I still cancel for free?
responses.response: Yes, you can cancel your booking for free within 48 hours from the time of booking, provided that your scheduled departure is not within 7 days. If you wish to proceed with the cancellation, please contact IndiGo's customer support for assistance.

responses.question: How long does a refund to my credit card take?
responses.response: For Bookings made through credit/debit cards or via wallets, the refund will be processed to the credit/debit card or to the wallet used to make such Booking within 7 (seven) working days from the date of the refund request.

responses.question: There's a spelling mistake in my name on the ticket. Can I fix it?
responses.response: Yes, you can correct a spelling mistake in your name within 24 (twenty-four) hours from the time of Booking without incurring any additional charges. You will need to contact IndiGo's Customer Care number at +91 124-4973838 / 124-6173838, provided the ticket was booked directly on the IndiGo website or mobile application.

responses.question: What happens to my money if I miss my flight without cancelling?
responses.response: If you do not check-in for a flight at least 60 (sixty) minutes prior to its scheduled time of departure, the entire amount paid for the Booking will be retained by IndiGo. However, you are entitled to a refund of Airport Charges, excluding GST.

responses.question: Is there an extra charge for cancelling through the call centre?
responses.response: The context does not specify whether there is an extra charge for cancelling through the call centre. I suggest contacting IndiGo support for more information on this matter.

responses.question: What is a Credit Shell?
responses.response: A Credit Shell is a credit note issued by IndiGo to a Customer, on account of any adjustment in the taxable value of services to be offered by IndiGo and any compensation to be provided to Customers under applicable law, which may include redressal of any Customer grievance in terms of the provisions of the Conditions of Carriage.

responses.question: How early should I reach the airport before my IndiGo flight?
responses.response: IndiGo recommends that customers report for check-in at IndiGo counters at least 2 (two) hours prior to the departure of the scheduled flight. This allows ample time to comply with the check-in requirements.

responses.question: Until when can I do web check-in?
responses.response: You can do web check-in on the IndiGo website anytime up to one (1) hour prior to the scheduled departure of your flight.

responses.question: Can I use my Aadhaar card as ID at check-in?
responses.response: Yes, you can use your Aadhaar or m-Aadhaar issued by the Unique Identification Authority of India ("UIDAI") as a valid identification document at check-in.

responses.question: Who is not allowed to sit in the emergency exit row?
responses.response: Customers who are not allowed to sit in the emergency exit row include those who are not able-bodied, are under 15 years of age on the date of travel, have a physical or mental condition that prevents them from opening an emergency exit, are suffering from a temporary illness affecting their mobility, are traveling with an infant, or are unwilling to assist in an emergency.

responses.question: My flight was overbooked and I was denied boarding. What am I entitled to?
responses.response: If you were denied boarding due to an overbooked flight, IndiGo will endeavor to seek volunteers first. If you were not a volunteer and were denied boarding, you may be entitled to compensation in the form of vouchers, unless IndiGo arranges a seat on an alternate flight scheduled to depart within 1 (one) hour of your original flight. Additionally, if you were on a connecting flight and arrived at your final destination at least 3 (three) hours later than scheduled due to denied boarding, you may be compensated for the first leg of your journey.

responses.question: Can I carry a power bank in my checked luggage?
responses.response: No, power banks and spare/loose lithium batteries are permitted only in hand baggage. They must remain within the passenger's personal reach at all times and must not be stowed in overhead compartments or checked baggage.

responses.question: How much alcohol can I carry in my checked bag?
responses.response: You may carry up to 5 litres of alcoholic beverages as part of your checked-in baggage, provided the alcoholic beverage is in retail packaging and packed appropriately to prevent damage or leakage. Additionally, the alcohol content must not exceed 70%. If the beverage contains 24% or less alcohol by volume, the 5-litre limitation does not apply.

responses.question: Can I bring my hoverboard on the flight?
responses.response: No, battery-operated hoverboards are not permitted as baggage on IndiGo flights.

responses.question: I want to carry a big LED TV back home. Is that allowed?
responses.response: You can carry a maximum of two (02) LCD/LED TVs in Checked-in Baggage, provided the maximum size of each TV does not exceed 139.7 centimetres including the cartons. Please ensure you comply with other applicable terms and conditions regarding the carriage of fragile items.

responses.question: Are e-cigarettes allowed on IndiGo flights?
responses.response: No, e-cigarettes are not permitted on IndiGo flights. Customers are not allowed to carry e-cigarettes in their hand baggage or checked-in baggage, and possession or use of e-cigarettes is prohibited on the premises of aerodromes and on the aircraft.

responses.question: My checked bag was lost by the airline. How much compensation can I get?
responses.response: For lost Checked-in Baggage, IndiGo's liability is limited to INR 350 per kg, subject to a maximum of INR 25,000 per passenger, unless a special declaration is made at the time of Check-in.

responses.question: My checked bag is at 13kg and I want to add a wine bottle. Domestic flight - will I go over the limit, and if yes what do I pay?
responses.response: For a domestic flight, the checked-in baggage allowance is 15 kg per person. If your checked bag is at 13 kg and you add a wine bottle, you may exceed the limit depending on the weight of the wine bottle. If you go over the limit, additional charges may apply for excess baggage, but the specific fees are not provided in the context. I recommend contacting IndiGo support for detailed information on excess baggage charges.

responses.question: I'm travelling with a 28kg checked bag. Does it matter whether my flight is to Dubai or to Nairobi?
responses.response: Yes, it matters. For flights to Dubai, the checked baggage allowance is 30kg per person, so your 28kg bag is within the limit. However, for flights to Nairobi, the allowance is only 25kg per person, which means your 28kg bag would exceed the limit and additional excess baggage charges may apply.

responses.question: I have two bags for my domestic trip and the second one counts as an extra piece. Do I save money by booking it online instead of paying at the airport?
responses.response: Yes, you can save money by pre-booking your additional piece of baggage online. The pre-booking charge is INR 800 per piece, while the charge at the airport is INR 1000 per piece. Therefore, booking online will save you INR 200.

responses.question: How much will 10kg of extra baggage cost me?
responses.response: For domestic travel, the cost for 10 kg of pre-paid excess baggage is ₹ 6,500. If you are at the airport, the charge is ₹ 700 per kg for excess baggage.
